prelim_notice_required: Varies by role; preliminary notice not generally required on private work

prelim_notice_required: Often; subs/suppliers typically send 30-day Notice to Contractor after receiving a Notice of Commencement; not always for GCs

enforcement_deadline: Suit to enforce within 1 year of filing (may be shortened by contest)

enforcement_deadline: Enforce within 180 days of last furnishing

prelim_notice_required: Yes (monthly fund-trapping notices for non-privity claimants)
prelim_notice_deadline: 2nd & 3rd month notices by the 15th day of the following month(s); complex by role/project type
lien_filing_deadline: Residential: 15th day of 3rd month; Commercial: 15th day of 4th month after last month of furnishing
enforcement_deadline: Generally within 1 year of the last date the lien could be filed; special rules apply
homestead_or_residential_note: Texas has unique monthly notice scheme; track each month’s unpaid work.

prelim_notice_required: Yes for many roles (e.g., Notice of Nonpayment/Intent); contract/termination notices can control deadlines
lien_filing_deadline: If Notice of Contract filed: 30 days after Notice of Termination; otherwise 60 days (varies by role)
enforcement_deadline: Varies by circumstance; consult current statute post-2020 updates
homestead_or_residential_note: Louisiana uses Notice of Contract/Termination framework; deadlines are triggered by filings.
tree_service_note: Emergency tree removal typically qualifies as labor for private property liens; verify contractor licensing & any municipal permitting.

enforcement_deadline: File suit within 180 days of filing (90 if owner contests)
homestead_or_residential_note: Residential pre-lien notice is required for subs.

prelim_notice_deadline: Notice of Intent at least 10 days before filing lien
